---
title: "Untitled"
format: html
---



```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(readr)
```


Dataframe

```{r}

df <- data.frame(
  YEAR = c("2024", "2024", "2024", "2024",
           "2025", "2025", "2025", "2025"),
  REG = c("Brazil", "Europe", "Brazil", "Europe",
             "Brazil", "Europe", "Brazil", "Europe"),
  PHASE = c("Arrival", "Arrival", "Departure", "Departure",
             "Arrival", "Arrival", "Departure", "Departure"),
  N = c(61.8, 57.1, 79.7, 60.6,
                  81.0, 77.3, 79.2, 76.1)
)




# real dataframe

## function to data preparation

add_dly_early_late_groupings2 <- function(.punc_df){
     df <- .punc_df |>
         dplyr::mutate(
             EARLY        = rowSums(across(.cols = `(-INF,-60]`:`(-20,-15]`), na.rm = TRUE)
             ,EARLY_M15M05 = (`(-15,-10]` + `(-10,-5]`)
             ,EARLY_M05M00 =  `(-5,0]`
             ,LATE_P00P05  =  `(0,5)`
             ,LATE_P05P15  = (`[5,10)` + `[10,15)`)
             ,LATE         = rowSums(across(.cols = `[15,20)`:`[60,INF)`), na.rm = TRUE)
             ,WITHIN_M05P05= (`(-5,0]` + `(0,5)`)
             ,WITHIN_M15P15= (`(-15,-10]`+`(-10,-5]`+`(-5,0]`+`(0,5)`+`[5,10)`+`[10,15)`)
         )
}

## Brazil

bra_punc <- read_csv(here::here("data" , "BRA-punc-2024.csv"))

 bra_punc |>
     group_by(PHASE) |>
     reframe(CHECK = n()
             , across(`(-INF,-60]`:`[60,INF)`, .fns = ~ sum(.x) )
     ) |>
     add_dly_early_late_groupings2() |>
     mutate(N = rowSums(across(`(-INF,-60]`:`[60,INF)`)) )|>
     mutate(across(.cols = EARLY:WITHIN_M15P15,.fns =  ~ .x / N) ) |> 
     select(PHASE , WITHIN_M15P15) |>
     mutate(REG = "BRA" , YEAR = "2024")
 
 
 
 ## Europe
 
 eur_punc <- read_csv(here::here("data" , "EUR-punc-2024.csv"))
 
  eur_punc |>
     group_by(PHASE) |>
     reframe(CHECK = n()
             , across(`(-INF,-60]`:`[60,INF)`, .fns = ~ sum(.x) )
     ) |>
     add_dly_early_late_groupings2() |>
     mutate(N = rowSums(across(`(-INF,-60]`:`[60,INF)`)) )|>
     mutate(across(.cols = EARLY:WITHIN_M15P15,.fns =  ~ .x / N) ) |> 
     select(PHASE , WITHIN_M15P15) |>
     mutate(REG = "EUR" , YEAR = "2024")

```



```{r}

plot_slope_punctuality_faceted <- function(
    data,
    region_var = "REG",
    value_var = "N",
    group_var = "YEAR",
    metric_var = "PHASE",
    title = "Brazil vs Europe punctuality comparison",
    subtitle = "Arrival and departure punctuality shown side by side"
) {
  
  df <- data |>
    dplyr::select(
      dplyr::all_of(region_var),
      dplyr::all_of(value_var),
      dplyr::all_of(group_var),
      dplyr::all_of(metric_var)
    ) |>
    dplyr::filter(
      !is.na(.data[[region_var]]),
      !is.na(.data[[value_var]]),
      !is.na(.data[[group_var]]),
      !is.na(.data[[metric_var]])
    ) |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      !!region_var := factor(.data[[region_var]], levels = c("Brazil", "Europe"))
    )
  
  # Keep only complete Brazil-Europe pairs
  valid_pairs <- df |>
    dplyr::distinct(
      .data[[metric_var]],
      .data[[group_var]],
      .data[[region_var]]
    ) |>
    dplyr::count(.data[[metric_var]], .data[[group_var]]) |>
    dplyr::filter(n == 2)
  
  df <- df |>
    dplyr::inner_join(
      valid_pairs,
      by = c(metric_var, group_var)
    ) |>
    dplyr::select(-n)
  
  # Data for labels
  df_left  <- df |> dplyr::filter(.data[[region_var]] == "Brazil")
  df_right <- df |> dplyr::filter(.data[[region_var]] == "Europe")
  
  ggplot2::ggplot(
    df,
    ggplot2::aes(
      x     = .data[[region_var]],
      y     = .data[[value_var]],
      group = .data[[group_var]],
      color = .data[[group_var]]
    )
  ) +
    ggplot2::geom_line(linewidth = 1.2, alpha = 0.85, lineend = "round") +
    ggplot2::geom_point(size = 3) +
    
    # Brazil labels — repelled to the left
    ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
      data          = df_left,
      ggplot2::aes(label = paste0(round(.data[[value_var]], 1), "%")),
      direction     = "y",          # only moves up/down to avoid overlap
      nudge_x       = -0.25,        # pushes labels to the left of the point
      hjust         = 1,
      size          = 3.5,
      segment.size  = 0.3,          # thin connecting line if label moves far
      segment.color = "grey60",
      show.legend   = FALSE
    ) +
    
    # Europe labels — repelled to the right
    ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
      data          = df_right,
      ggplot2::aes(label = paste0(round(.data[[value_var]], 1), "%")),
      direction     = "y",
      nudge_x       = 0.25,
      hjust         = 0,
      size          = 3.5,
      segment.size  = 0.3,
      segment.color = "grey60",
      show.legend   = FALSE
    ) +
    
    ggplot2::facet_wrap(
      stats::as.formula(paste("~", metric_var)),
      nrow = 1
    ) +
    
    ggplot2::scale_x_discrete(
      expand = ggplot2::expansion(mult = c(0.25, 0.25))
    ) +
    
    ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(
      limits = c(0, 100),
      breaks = seq(0, 100, 10),
      labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%"),
      expand = c(0, 0)
    ) +
    
    ggplot2::coord_cartesian(clip = "off") +
    
    ggplot2::labs(
      title    = title,
      subtitle = subtitle,
      x        = NULL,
      y        = "Punctuality (%)",
      color    = "Year",
      caption  = "Source: DECEA Performance Section & EUROCONTROL PRU"
    ) +
    
    ggplot2::scale_color_manual(
  values = c("2024" = "#5BBCB0", "2025" = "blue")
    ) +
    
    ggplot2::theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    ggplot2::theme(
      panel.grid.minor      = ggplot2::element_blank(),
      panel.grid.major.x    = ggplot2::element_blank(),
      axis.text.x           = ggplot2::element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"),
      strip.text            = ggplot2::element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
      plot.title            = ggplot2::element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
      plot.subtitle         = ggplot2::element_text(size = 11),
      legend.position       = "bottom",
      plot.margin           = ggplot2::margin(t = 15, r = 35, b = 15, l = 35)
    )
}
```





```{r}

ggplot2::ggsave(
  filename = "slope_arrival_departure_brazil_europe.png",
  plot = p,
  width = 12,
  height = 8,
  dpi = 300,
  bg = "white"
)


p <- plot_slope_punctuality_faceted(df)

```
